meta.focus_summary_text:
🔥 Weekly AP Quant Radar — Technical Focus List

[ 📉 BREAKDOWNS (Below 200-SMA) ]
• $POWL | RISK_OFF | BD200+HIGH_RVOL+EVENT | -41.9% | Vol 3.08x | Powell Industries Announces Three-For-One Stock Split
• $APP | TRIM | BD200+BUY_RATED+DVG | -20.5% | Vol 0.63x | AppLovin to Announce First Quarter 2026 Results
• $CRDO | WATCH | BD200+STRONG_BUY+DVG | -18.3% | Vol 0.60x | Why Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd's (CRDO) Stock Is Up 6.24%
• $CCL | TRIM | BD200+POS_NEWS+RISK_OFF | -12.5% | Vol 0.79x | Carnival Corporation & plc Achieves Record First Quarter Operating Results and Record Bookings
• $RCL | HOLD_STRONG | BD200+POS_NEWS+EVENT | -11.2% | Vol 0.89x | Royal Caribbean Fuel Hedge Reshapes Cruise Line Cost and Margin Story
• $W | WATCH | BD200+EVENT | -17.9% | Vol 1.06x | Wayfair Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
• $SKYW | WATCH | BD200+NEAR_EMA21+EVENT | -10.3% | Vol 0.81x | SkyWest, Inc. Announces First Quarter 2026 Results Call Date
• $OKTA | WATCH | BD200+BUY_RATED+DVG | -10.0% | Vol 1.10x | Why Okta (OKTA) Is Up 6.7% After Bullish Calls Highlight Its AI Opportunity

● Recovery Triggers (EMA21 reclaim):
  • POWL > 398.08
  • APP > 421.61
  • CRDO > 103.95
  • CCL > 25.93
  • RCL > 277.45
  • W > 75.11
  • SKYW > 92.91
  • OKTA > 78.69

Full dashboard view :https://wid1990-ap.streamlit.app/

focus_view_model.summary_headline: Catastrophic breakdown on 3.08x RVOL overwhelms the fresh stock-split catalyst. ([Powell Industries, Inc.][1])

external_intel.headline: Powell Industries Announces Three-For-One Stock Split
external_intel.summary: Powell’s split-adjusted trading began on April 6, 2026, but the chart damage is so severe that the corporate catalyst has not translated into price support. ([Powell Industries, Inc.][1])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $201.70 is below EMA21 $398.08, EMA55 $442.24, and SMA200 $347.21; RSI14 at 22.3 confirms an extreme oversold breakdown, and the -41.9% bias to SMA200 is the worst in the set.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 3.08x is the strongest conviction signal in the pack and confirms active institutional distribution rather than low-volume drift.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: There is a fresh corporate catalyst, but it is a stock-split event rather than an earnings or guidance repair; price action is dominating the narrative. ([Powell Industries, Inc.][1])

divergence_audit.note: QuantRating is only Hold, so the breakdown is broadly aligned with the local rating profile even though the magnitude of the collapse is exceptional.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: EXIT while price remains below SMA200 $347.21 | Secondary: any repair must first reclaim EMA21 $398.08, then EMA55 $442.24 | Failure: continued trade below LatestPrice $201.70 confirms unresolved breakdown | Next Action: de-risk until a verified multi-average repair appears

panel.one_line_verdict: RISK_OFF — split catalyst failed to arrest a high-conviction structural breakdown.

evidence.catalyst_status: 3-for-1 split catalyst active, with split-adjusted trading beginning April 6, 2026. ([Powell Industries, Inc.][1])

playbook.trigger: Primary: reclaim EMA21 $398.08 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $442.24 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $347.21

focus_view_model.summary_headline: Buy-rated APP remains broken below all major averages ahead of its next earnings date. ([investors.applovin.com][2])

external_intel.headline: AppLovin to Announce First Quarter 2026 Results
external_intel.summary: AppLovin scheduled Q1 results for May 6, 2026, while recent coverage also pointed to stronger Q1 ad trends and a higher price target from Wells Fargo, creating a clear rating/news versus price divergence. ([investors.applovin.com][2])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $410.44 is below EMA21 $421.61, EMA55 $462.82, and SMA200 $516.50, so the stock remains in a confirmed breakdown despite only a mildly oversold RSI14 of 37.4.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 0.63x shows no meaningful accumulation; the stock is not attracting strong rescue buying at current levels.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: The earnings date is fresh and some recent external commentary is constructive, but the market is still discounting APP as a broken chart rather than a repaired growth leader. ([investors.applovin.com][2])

divergence_audit.note: QuantRating is Buy at 4.36 while price is below EMA21, EMA55, and SMA200; that is a textbook rating-price divergence.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: REDUCE while price remains below SMA200 $516.50 | Secondary: first repair trigger is reclaim of EMA21 $421.61, then EMA55 $462.82 | Failure: rejection back through LatestPrice $410.44 keeps the breakdown active | Next Action: wait for a verified reclaim of EMA21 $421.61 before upgrading stance

panel.one_line_verdict: BREAKDOWN — external optimism exists, but the chart still has not repaired.

evidence.catalyst_status: Q1 2026 results scheduled for May 6, 2026; recent channel checks and target revisions were constructive. ([investors.applovin.com][2])

playbook.trigger: Primary: reclaim EMA21 $421.61 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $462.82 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $516.50

focus_view_model.summary_headline: Strong Buy CRDO is above EMA21, but the larger SMA200 breakdown is still unresolved. ([aaii.com][3])

external_intel.headline: Why Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd's (CRDO) Stock Is Up 6.24%
external_intel.summary: Fresh commentary points to ongoing analyst support and favorable valuation framing for Credo, but the stock still needs to prove it can repair the larger trend damage. ([aaii.com][3])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $106.79 is above EMA21 $103.95, but still below EMA55 $115.12 and SMA200 $130.71, so this is an early repair attempt inside a broader breakdown.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 0.60x is weak for a true trend reversal; the bounce lacks the volume signature of a decisive institutional reclaim.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: External sentiment remains constructive, but the technical structure still says 'divergence watch' rather than 'confirmed recovery.' ([aaii.com][3])

divergence_audit.note: QuantRating is Strong Buy at 4.82 while the stock remains -18.3% below SMA200 and below EMA55.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: WATCH while price holds above EMA21 $103.95 | Secondary: stronger confirmation requires reclaim of EMA55 $115.12, then SMA200 $130.71 | Failure: loss of EMA21 $103.95 invalidates the early repair attempt | Next Action: keep stance at WATCH until SMA200 $130.71 is recovered

panel.one_line_verdict: DIVERGENCE — fundamentals and ratings are stronger than the current chart.

evidence.catalyst_status: Fresh bullish commentary and analyst support remain in place, but price has not yet repaired the higher averages. ([aaii.com][3])

playbook.trigger: Primary: hold EMA21 $103.95 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $115.12 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $130.71

focus_view_model.summary_headline: Record Q1 results were not enough to offset oil-sensitive macro pressure and weak structure. ([Carnival Corporation][4])

external_intel.headline: Carnival Corporation & plc Achieves Record First Quarter Operating Results and Record Bookings
external_intel.summary: Carnival reported record first-quarter results and bookings, but management also explicitly referenced higher fuel prices, leaving the stock exposed in a macro tape that is rewarding defensives over fuel-sensitive travel names. ([Carnival Corporation][4])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $25.20 is below EMA21 $25.93, EMA55 $27.48, and SMA200 $28.79, so the earnings beat has not translated into a technical repair.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 0.79x shows the stock is not drawing strong follow-through demand despite a favorable company print.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: Company-specific results are good, but regime context matters: SPY is near flat while risk appetite remains selective and fuel-sensitive cruise operators are carrying a macro handicap. ([Carnival Corporation][4])

divergence_audit.note: Positive earnings and bookings conflict with a still-broken chart and a macro backdrop that is unfavorable for oil-sensitive leisure names.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: REDUCE while price remains below SMA200 $28.79 | Secondary: first repair requires reclaim of EMA21 $25.93, then EMA55 $27.48 | Failure: continued trade below LatestPrice $25.20 keeps downside pressure active | Next Action: maintain risk-off stance until price repairs above EMA55 $27.48

panel.one_line_verdict: MACRO CONFLICT — strong company news is still being overruled by weak structure.

evidence.catalyst_status: Record Q1 2026 results and bookings, but management flagged higher fuel prices as a live variable. ([Carnival Corporation][4])

playbook.trigger: Primary: reclaim EMA21 $25.93 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $27.48 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $28.79

focus_view_model.summary_headline: RCL remains below trend, but fuel hedging gives it a cleaner setup than cruise peers. ([simplywall.st][5])

external_intel.headline: Royal Caribbean Fuel Hedge Reshapes Cruise Line Cost and Margin Story
external_intel.summary: Recent coverage emphasized that Royal Caribbean has hedged about 60% of 2026 fuel needs, giving it a relative earnings cushion versus cruise peers in the current oil backdrop. ([simplywall.st][5])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $267.71 is below EMA21 $277.45, EMA55 $286.82, and SMA200 $301.31, so the stock remains in breakdown status despite better relative fundamentals.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 0.89x is not a high-conviction accumulation signal, but it is also not a panic liquidation profile.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: Compared with other cruise names, RCL’s fuel hedging is a real relative-strength support in a macro regime where oil sensitivity matters. ([simplywall.st][5])

divergence_audit.note: The stock remains technically broken even though its external catalyst set is stronger than most direct cruise peers.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: HOLD while monitoring for reclaim of EMA21 $277.45 | Secondary: stronger repair requires reclaim of EMA55 $286.82, then SMA200 $301.31 | Failure: inability to hold LatestPrice $267.71 would weaken the relative-strength thesis | Next Action: retain HOLD rather than REDUCE because relative catalyst quality is better than peers

panel.one_line_verdict: RELATIVE HOLD — broken chart, but a better external setup than the rest of cruise.

evidence.catalyst_status: Recent coverage highlighted roughly 60% hedging of 2026 fuel needs. ([simplywall.st][5])

playbook.trigger: Primary: reclaim EMA21 $277.45 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $286.82 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $301.31

focus_view_model.summary_headline: Wayfair has a fresh earnings date, but the chart is still materially below every key trend marker. ([investor.wayfair.com][6])

external_intel.headline: Wayfair Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
external_intel.summary: Wayfair scheduled Q1 2026 results for April 30, 2026, which makes the name event-relevant, but there is no fresh positive catalyst strong enough to offset the technical damage. ([investor.wayfair.com][6])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $69.78 is below EMA21 $75.11, EMA55 $82.34, and SMA200 $85.02, leaving a clear breakdown structure with a -17.9% bias to SMA200.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 1.06x is close to normal and does not confirm either capitulation or a strong repair bid.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: The fresh earnings-calendar item lifts watch value, but not enough to override the current breakdown. ([investor.wayfair.com][6])

divergence_audit.note: QuantRating is only Hold, so the current price structure is broadly aligned with the local rating profile.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: REDUCE while price remains below SMA200 $85.02 | Secondary: first repair requires reclaim of EMA21 $75.11, then EMA55 $82.34 | Failure: rejection back through LatestPrice $69.78 preserves the breakdown | Next Action: maintain defensive stance into the April 30 earnings event

panel.one_line_verdict: EVENT WATCH — calendar relevance exists, but structure remains decisively weak.

evidence.catalyst_status: Q1 2026 results are scheduled for April 30, 2026. ([investor.wayfair.com][6])

playbook.trigger: Primary: reclaim EMA21 $75.11 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $82.34 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $85.02

focus_view_model.summary_headline: SKYW is near EMA21 with a fresh earnings date, but the broader 200-day break is still open. ([inc.skywest.com][7])

external_intel.headline: SkyWest, Inc. Announces First Quarter 2026 Results Call Date
external_intel.summary: SkyWest scheduled its Q1 2026 results call for April 23, 2026, which creates a legitimate near-term catalyst but not a confirmed trend reversal. ([inc.skywest.com][7])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $92.63 is just under EMA21 $92.91 and still below EMA55 $96.25 and SMA200 $103.21, making this a routine-maintenance chart rather than a repaired one.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 0.81x is light, so there is no evidence of strong sponsorship ahead of the event.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: The earnings date is fresh enough to keep SKYW in the top eight, but the setup is still mostly a watchlist maintenance case. ([inc.skywest.com][7])

divergence_audit.note: QuantRating is Hold and price remains below all three moving averages, so there is no severe rating-price mismatch.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: WATCH for reclaim of EMA21 $92.91 | Secondary: stronger repair requires reclaim of EMA55 $96.25, then SMA200 $103.21 | Failure: continued rejection below EMA21 $92.91 keeps the name in maintenance mode | Next Action: remain observational into the April 23 event

panel.one_line_verdict: ROUTINE MAINTENANCE — event relevance exists, but the chart is not repaired.

evidence.catalyst_status: Q1 2026 results call scheduled for April 23, 2026. ([inc.skywest.com][7])

playbook.trigger: Primary: reclaim EMA21 $92.91 | Secondary: reclaim EMA55 $96.25 | Failure: continued trade below SMA200 $103.21

focus_view_model.summary_headline: OKTA is stabilizing above EMA21, but the Buy rating still leads the broken 200-day chart. ([Investing.com][8])

external_intel.headline: Why Okta (OKTA) Is Up 6.7% After Bullish Calls Highlight Its AI Opportunity
external_intel.summary: Recent commentary highlighted renewed bullish calls around Okta’s AI-security positioning, while official quarterly materials still point to stable execution and cash available to repay the remaining 2026 Notes. ([simplywall.st][9])

logic_pillars.technical: LatestPrice $79.34 is above EMA21 $78.69, but still below EMA55 $80.76 and SMA200 $88.17, so this remains an early repair setup within a longer breakdown.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: RVOL20 at 1.10x is firmer than most names in the pack, but still below the 1.15 threshold needed for a true volume alert.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: The external tone has improved, but the chart still needs to close the gap between a Buy-rated narrative and an unresolved SMA200 break. ([simplywall.st][9])

divergence_audit.note: QuantRating is Buy at 4.03 while the stock remains below EMA55 and SMA200.

focus_view_model.action_plan: Primary: WATCH while price holds above EMA21 $78.69 | Secondary: stronger confirmation requires reclaim of EMA55 $80.76, then SMA200 $88.17 | Failure: loss of EMA21 $78.69 weakens the repair case | Next Action: keep stance at WATCH until SMA200 $88.17 is recovered

panel.one_line_verdict: EARLY REPAIR — improving setup, but the higher-trend repair is still incomplete.
